suggestedPatch.target: Coordinating a group trip to Oahu only works when you combine a real itinerary, brutal honesty about budget, and a shared plan for chaos control.
suggestedPatch.before: Coordinating a group trip to Oahu only works when you combine a real itinerary, brutal honesty about budget, and a shared plan for chaos control.
suggestedPatch.after: Our group only stopped bickering once we'd booked Hanauma Bay, assigned a car driver, and agreed Sarah wasn't picking dinner anymore.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Mostly reads like a listicle template with scattered attempts at personality; tone shifts suggest multiple passes or AI generation.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 5/10 — Has some light opinions (Ala Moana beats Waikiki) but mostly hedges and repeats safe travel-blog consensus.

suggestedPatch.target: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
suggestedPatch.before: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip Diamond Head sunrise—the parking scramble ruins it. Do the late-afternoon slot instead, when the light's better and half the tour buses have left.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 5/10: Has some light opinions (Ala Moana beats Waikiki) but mostly hedges and repeats safe travel-blog consensus.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — A few lively bits ('one organiser, seven opinions, 1000 unread messages') are diluted by generic filler.

suggestedPatch.after: Keep this—it's one of the few lines with bite. The rest should match: 'Rideshare dies the moment you leave Honolulu' beats 'Rideshare is hit-or-miss outside Honolulu.'
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: A few lively bits ('one organiser, seven opinions, 1000 unread messages') are diluted by generic filler.

evidence: Point of view 6/10 — Takes sides on lodging and timing but often lapses into survey-mode FAQs.

suggestedPatch.target: Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids.
suggestedPatch.before: Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids.
suggestedPatch.after: If you're bringing kids, this isn't the itinerary—our version assumes late dinners, Bar 35, and nobody asking about naptime.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view" scored 6/10: Takes sides on lodging and timing but often lapses into survey-mode FAQs.

evidence: Quotable sentence 5/10 — One line (the Hanauma Bay moment) is genuinely quotable; most others are forgettable.

suggestedPatch.target: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos.
suggestedPatch.before: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos.
suggestedPatch.after: There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay and you realize nobody booked it.

suggestedPatch.before: Remove this meta-instruction entirely; replace with a concrete opener like 'Last May, five of us landed at HNL with zero bookings and one shared Note.'
suggestedPatch.after: Last May, five of us landed at HNL with zero bookings, one shared Note, and a group chat already 400 messages deep.

evidence: intro_hook 4/10 — The actual post opener (ignoring the TL;DR meta-block) is generic throat-clearing. 'Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos' is a platitude that signals nothing specific. The post itself contains a better seed ('There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay and you realize nobody booked it') but buries it several paragraphs in. The opener needs a concrete scene, a named place, a number, or a personal failure — not a category statement.

suggestedPatch.target: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos. Here's a day-by-day, friend-proof 7-day itinerary using real spots, plus what actually matters for planning.
suggestedPatch.before: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos. Here's a day-by-day, friend-proof 7-day itinerary using real spots, plus what actually matters for planning.
suggestedPatch.after: Last May, five of us landed at HNL with zero bookings and one shared Notes doc. By day two, we'd lost half a morning to a full Hanauma Bay parking lot and a 45-minute plate-lunch debate. Here's the itinerary we wish we'd had.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 4/10: The actual post opener (ignoring the TL;DR meta-block) is generic throat-clearing. 'Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos' is a platitude that signals nothing specific. The post itself contains a better seed ('There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay and you realize nobody booked it') but buries it several paragraphs in. The opener needs a concrete scene, a named place, a number, or a personal failure — not a category statement.
